specific_mechanism: Thixotropic / yield-stress fluids exhibit time-dependent viscosity: apparent viscosity drops with sustained shear and recovers when shear stops; the recovery time and the yield-stress threshold are distinct parameters. Below yield stress the fluid is rigid; above, the time-dependent shear-thinning kicks in
llm_application: Implement an LLM 'yield-stress gate' on activation updates: an activation does not respond to small input perturbations (below yield), but past a threshold the response time-thins (the activation becomes increasingly malleable with sustained pushing); when push stops, the activation slowly recovers its baseline rigidity. The mechanism is dual-parameter (yield-stress threshold + recovery time) and gives a structural defense against gradient-noise drift while allowing intentional updates.
novelty_claim: Existing activation update / gradient clipping uses scalar magnitude thresholds. No published 2024-2026 LLM/AI work formalizes a thixotropic yield-stress gate with separate yield-stress and recovery-time parameters on activation updates.
